title: lupinus cusickii subsp. cusickii
intro: Lupinus cusickii subsp. cusickii, also known as Cusick's lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to create herbal remedies, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lupinus cusickii subsp. cusickii include its potential to reduce pain and swelling, as well as its ability to support the immune system and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
